# Reconstruction Report — pymetafor

Reference-driven port of **`metafor::rma.uni`** (Viechtbauer 2010, JSS) under the
Omicverse-RebuildR protocol.

## 1. Identity
| | |
|---|---|
| Package | `pymetafor` (socialverse/external) |
| Upstream | metafor **5.0.1** (R 4.5.2) |
| Function ported | `rma()` — random / mixed / equal-effects meta-analysis (rma.uni) |
| Algorithm class | 1 (deterministic numerical) for pooled fits; τ²-tolerance-bounded for meta-regression |
| Threshold | `max_abs_err < 1e-6` (protocol hard ceiling), committed before porting |
| Final parity | 1e-6 on all intercept-only outputs; meta-regression to metafor's own convergence bound |
| LOC | ~230 (pure numpy/scipy) |

## 2. R function coverage
| metafor feature | ported | parity |
|---|---|---|
| REML τ² (Fisher scoring) | ✅ | 1e-6 |
| DL τ² (closed form) | ✅ | 1e-6 |
| ML τ² | ✅ | (gated via REML/DL fixtures) |
| Equal-effects (EE/FE) | ✅ | 1e-6 |
| β, SE(β), z, p, CI | ✅ | 1e-6 |
| Knapp-Hartung (`test="knha"`) t-adjustment | ✅ | 1e-6 |
| I², H² (Higgins-Thompson s²) | ✅ | 1e-6 |
| Q_E, Q_Ep (heterogeneity) | ✅ | 1e-6 (τ²-independent → exact 1e-13) |
| SE(τ²) (inverse Fisher info) | ✅ | 1e-6 |
| Meta-regression (moderators), Q_M | ✅ | slopes+Q_E 1e-6; τ² to metafor tol |
| Prediction interval (`predict.rma`) | ✅ | 1e-6 |
| PM/SJ/HS/HE τ² estimators | ⬜ deferred | (socialverse legacy path retained) |
| rma.mv (multilevel) | ⬜ next increment | (socialverse `_reml_3level` retained) |

## 3. Parity evidence
Canonical fixture: `metafor::escalc(measure="RR", …, data=dat.bcg)` → k=13 log risk
ratios. R reference generated by `tests/r_reference_driver.R`; gate in
`tests/test_parity.py` (**8/8 passing**). Representative element-wise errors vs
metafor 5.0.1:

| output (REML) | max_abs_err |
|---|---|
| β (pooled logRR) | 1.6e-9 |
| SE(β) | <1e-8 |
| τ² | 5e-8 |
| I² | 3.9e-7 |
| Q_E | 1.1e-13 |
| SE(τ²) | <1e-7 |
| prediction interval | <1e-7 |

Reproduce: `Rscript pymetafor/tests/r_reference_driver.R && python -m pytest pymetafor/tests/test_parity.py`.

## 4. Acceleration
None required (k is small; the fit is O(k²) per Fisher step). The one algebraic
choice made for **correctness** (not speed): the projection `P = W^½(I−QQᵀ)W^½`
is built from the thin QR of the weight-scaled design rather than an explicit
`(XᵀWX)⁻¹`, so the port stays accurate at `cond(XᵀWX) ≈ 2e11` (uncentred
moderators). Admissibility: **(E) exact identity** (QR factorisation of the same
normal equations).

## 5. Code quality
- `python -m pytest pymetafor/tests/test_parity.py` → 8 passed.
- socialverse `tests/test_meta.py` → 17 passed with `meta_random` delegating to pymetafor.
- pure numpy/scipy; no runtime R/rpy2.

## 6. Known limitations
- **Meta-regression τ² is bounded by metafor's own convergence tolerance, not by
  this port.** metafor's Fisher scoring stops at its default `threshold=1e-5`, so
  the τ² it *reports* for a flat/ill-conditioned objective can sit up to ~1e-5
  from the exact REML root. On the `dat.bcg ~ ablat + year` fit (`cond≈2e11`,
  `year` uncentred, intercept SE≈29 → unidentified), this propagates to ~4e-4 on
  the intercept. Verified independently: the exact REML root (Brent + argmax)
  differs from metafor's reported τ² by 2.65e-6 — i.e. the residual is metafor's
  under-convergence, not a port error. The **identified** quantities (moderator
  slopes, Q_E, Q_M) match metafor to 1e-6 / its convergence bound; centring the
  moderators removes the intercept conditioning entirely. pymetafor replicates
  metafor's `threshold=1e-5` so its *reported* numbers track metafor's.
- PM/SJ/HS/HE τ² estimators and `rma.mv` (multilevel) are not yet ported;
  socialverse keeps its existing implementations for those paths.

## 7. Integration into socialverse
- Vendored at `socialverse/external/pymetafor`.
- `socialverse.tl._meta.meta_random` delegates REML/ML/DL/EE pooling to
  `pymetafor.rma` (and now surfaces I²/H²/Q_E/SE(τ²)/prediction-interval).
- Live check: `sv.tl.meta_random(..., method="REML")` matches metafor's pooled
  estimate to 1.6e-9 on the BCG fixture.

## 8. Sign-off
First port under the protocol; establishes the `external/` template (R reference
driver → port → committed parity gate → socialverse wire-in). Audit class **A**
(translation-only) for the pooled fit.
